evals.name: klaviyo-simple-to-canary-migration
evals.prompt: Upgrade Klaviyo destination from version 2025-01-15 to 2026-01-15. The destination currently uses a simple REVISION_DATE constant in config.ts without feature flags. Please migrate it to use the canary pattern with feature flags, analyze breaking changes from the Klaviyo API changelog (https://developers.klaviyo.com/en/docs/changelog), run all tests, and create a PR. The Klaviyo API uses the revision date as an HTTP header.
evals.expected_output:
Complete upgrade with:
- versioning-info.ts created with both versions
- config.ts updated to import from versioning-info
- buildHeaders() updated to accept features parameter
- getApiRevision() helper function created
- extendRequest updated to pass features
- All tests passing for both versions
- Breaking changes analysis document
- PR created with detailed description
- Branch pushed to origin

evals.name: google-cm360-canary-update
evals.prompt: hey can you bump Google Campaign Manager 360 from v4 to v5? they already have the canary pattern set up with versioning-info.ts and feature flags. just need to update the CANARY version from v5 to v6 and check for breaking changes. their changelog is at https://developers.google.com/doubleclick-advertisers/deprecation. run tests and push a pr when done
evals.expected_output:
Complete upgrade with:
- GOOGLE_CM360_CANARY_API_VERSION updated to 'v6'
- GOOGLE_CM360_API_VERSION remains 'v4' (stable)
- Breaking changes fetched from Google docs
- Comprehensive analysis of v4→v6 changes
- All tests passing (stable and canary)
- Feature flag tests verified
- PR created with breaking changes
- Branch created and pushed

evals.name: new-destination-first-version
evals.prompt: We're adding a new destination called 'amplitude-web' that uses API version 'v2' from https://amplitude.com/api/v2. It currently has the version hardcoded in utils.ts as a constant. Can you set up the versioning structure properly with v1 as stable and v2 as canary behind a feature flag? Also check https://amplitude.com/docs/apis/api-changelog for breaking changes between v1 and v2.
evals.expected_output:
Complete setup with:
- versioning-info.ts created from scratch
- AMPLITUDE_WEB_API_VERSION = 'v1'
- AMPLITUDE_WEB_CANARY_API_VERSION = 'v2'
- getApiVersion() helper created
- FLAGON_NAME defined
- All API call sites updated to use helper
- Tests added for both versions
- Breaking changes analysis
- PR with full implementation
